☁
Future Considerations
Planned features and enhancements for the SEA Fulfillment Hub
✦
AI-Powered Summaries
Generate plain-English performance summaries from your live data. Choose from weekly recap, action items, movers & trends, or stakeholder email format. One click, ready to share.
Requires Netlify Edge Function setup for CORS. API key stored securely as environment variable.
📈
Year-on-Year Comparison
Compare the same week or quarter across years. W07 2026 vs W07 2025 side by side, per store. The data structure already supports it — just needs a YoY view layer.
Available once 12+ months of data has been loaded. Recommended to compare at KPI level rather than composite scores.
🔍
Store Benchmarking
Cross-country benchmarking to compare equivalent stores in MY, SG and TH. Identify which market sets the standard and share best practice across the SEA network.
Requires consistent store ID mapping across markets.
🔔
Automated Alerts & Digest
Weekly email or Teams digest sent automatically after upload. Highlights Tier D stores, missed revenue, and biggest movers without needing to open the dashboard.
Requires a webhook or scheduled function — Netlify or a simple automation tool like Zapier.
🎯
Store Targets & Recovery Plans
Set individual store targets beyond the network-wide claimed target. Track recovery plans for Tier D stores with a target score and deadline, visible in the store drawer.
Would extend the cnc_notes and sfs_notes tables with target and deadline columns.
📋
Programme Expansion
Add additional fulfilment programmes beyond SFS and C&C — for example Same Day Delivery or Returns performance — using the same country tab and scoring architecture.
Each new programme would need its own Supabase tables and CSV parser.
How to request a feature
Raise it in your next session with Claude. The dashboard codebase is preserved across sessions — any of the above can be built incrementally without rebuilding from scratch. The data structure and Supabase setup are already in place for most features.
JD Sports SEA — Fulfilment Hub
How to use this dashboard  ·  Ship From Store & Click & Collect  ·  MY · SG · TH
🏪What is this?
▶

The JD Sports SEA Fulfilment Hub is a weekly performance dashboard that tracks how well stores across Malaysia, Singapore and Thailand are fulfilling online customer orders. It covers two fulfilment programmes and turns raw Onestock data into ranked store scorecards, network health charts, automated alerts and AI-generated summaries — all in one place.

📦 SFS — Ship From Store
Store staff pick and despatch online orders directly from the shop floor. The hub scores each store weekly on how often they claim orders, how reliably they fulfil them, how fast they act, and how many orders they process per day.
🛍️ C&C — Click & Collect
Customers buy online and collect in store. The hub tracks whether orders are being claimed and bagged, how long it takes, and critically — how much revenue is being lost to unclaimed or unavailable orders.
Live database: All data lives in Supabase (cloud). Every upload is instantly visible to all users across all countries and programmes — no refresh needed.

Who manages it: Data is uploaded by admins each week. Scoring weights and tier thresholds are configurable by admins. The underlying data comes from Onestock's BI Suite.
👥Who is it for?
▶
RoleHow they use itKey features
Ecommerce & Ops Managers Monitor network performance across all three markets. Spot declining stores before they become a problem. Build weekly narrative for leadership. Movers, Alerts, AI summaries, Network health charts, Period comparison
Country Leads (MY / SG / TH) View their market in isolation. Compare this week vs last week or across a full quarter. Open individual store drawers to investigate and leave notes. Stores table, Store drawer, Quarter/Month filters, Overall view
Admins Upload weekly CSV data for each country and programme. Adjust scoring weights and tier thresholds. Delete incorrect uploads. Manage saved configurations. CSV upload, Score weight sliders, Config save/delete, Week delete
Tip: The dashboard is designed to be read top-down. Start with the Alerts tab to see anything urgent, then move to Movers to understand who drove this week's changes, then use the Stores table for a full ranked view.
🔐Getting in — Login
▶

When you open the hub, a branded login screen appears before any data is visible. You need a Supabase account to access it — contact your admin to be provisioned. Your session stays active until you close or refresh the tab.

1
Open the HTML file in Chrome, Edge or Safari
2
Enter your Supabase email & password on the login screen
3
Dashboard loads — select your country and programme tab
⚠ Two separate passwords — don't confuse them
🔑 Supabase login Your personal email + password for the dashboard. Set up in Supabase Authentication. This is what you type on the login screen. Contact your admin if you don't have one.
🛠 Admin password A separate built-in password used inside the dashboard to unlock scoring sliders, save scoring configs, and delete data. It is not your Supabase login. See the Scores & Tiers section for the password and a list of what it unlocks.
🗂️Navigating the dashboard
▶

The dashboard is structured in three layers. Always start at the top — pick your country, then your programme, then the view you want.

LayerOptionsWhat it does
Country tabs🇲🇾 Malaysia   🇸🇬 Singapore   🇹🇭 ThailandEach country is completely independent — separate uploads, separate scoring, separate history
Programme tabsShip From Store   Click & CollectSwitch between the two fulfilment programmes within a country
View tabsStores · Health · Movers · Alerts · OverallDifferent angles on the same data — ranked table, network trend, movers, flags, and long-run averages
Time period controls
ControlHow to use it
Quarter filterSelect Q1–Q4 to jump straight to all weeks in that quarter. Automatically switches to Period compare mode.
Month filterSelect a calendar month to view all weeks within it. Clears the quarter filter automatically.
Week dropdownIn Single week mode — pick any individual week. In Period compare mode — Ctrl/Cmd+click to select multiple weeks.
Single weekScores and ranks all stores for one specific week. Shows deltas vs the previous week.
Period compareAverages performance across a selected range of weeks and compares it to the equivalent prior period. Great for monthly or quarterly reviews.
Quick nav tip: The fastest way to jump to a month is to use the Month dropdown — it auto-selects all weeks in that month and switches you to period view. Use Quarter for a broader view.
📤Uploading weekly data
▶

Data is uploaded once per week, per country, per programme. Each upload adds a new week to the live database — visible to all users immediately. Data comes from Onestock BI Suite.

1
Type the week label in the input box
2
Wait for the green ✓ to confirm valid format
3
Click Upload SFS CSV or Upload C&C CSV
4
Select the exported CSV from Onestock
5
Wait for the Saved ✓ toast confirmation
Week label format: Must be W followed by the week number (1–52), a space, then the 4-digit year.
✓ Valid: W1 2026    W9 2026    W12 2026    W52 2026 ✗ Invalid: WEEK 1 2026    W53 2026    w12 2026    W1 26    2026 W12

The upload button stays disabled until a valid label is entered. If a week already exists, you'll be asked to confirm before overwriting. After a successful upload, an Undo button appears for 10 minutes.
Where to find the exports in Onestock
SFS — Ship From Store
1
Log in to Onestock
2
Go to BI Suite
3
Navigate to Competitive Analysis
4
Open "Orders to claim vs claimed orders"
5
Set the date range to match the week being uploaded
6
Export as CSV
C&C — Click & Collect
1
Log in to Onestock
2
Go to BI Suite
3
Navigate to Performance: Click and Collect
4
Open the Bag Performance report
5
Set the date range to match the week being uploaded
6
Export as CSV
⚠ Important: The date range in Onestock must exactly match the week you're uploading. Uploading the wrong date range will silently load incorrect data. Always double-check before uploading.
📊What each view tab shows
▶
TabWhat you seeBest used for
Stores Full ranked table of every store — rank, composite score, tier badge, claimed %, reliability, avg TTC, orders per day, and a sparkline trend. Click any row to open a store drawer with full week-by-week history, a score trend chart, root cause selector and notes. Weekly review, comparing stores, adding notes, identifying outliers
Network health Time-series chart of the network average score across all loaded weeks. Colour-coded by tier. Shows how many stores improved vs declined vs stayed flat week-on-week. For C&C also shows average time-to-bag trend. Spotting macro trends, presenting network direction to leadership
Movers Top 5 most improved and top 5 most declined stores based on score delta vs the previous week. Shows tier changes (e.g. C → B) and note dots if a store has been annotated. Quick identification of who's driving change this week
Alerts Auto-generated flags updated each week. Covers: tier drops, score falls over 15pts, stores below the claimed target, stores missing from uploads, 3+ consecutive weeks in Tier D, and network-wide floor breaches. Filterable by week. Severity: High or Medium. First tab to check every week — catches anything that needs action
Overall All-time average view across every week loaded. Shows avg score, avg claimed %, avg reliability, avg speed, trend (first vs latest week), and how many weeks of data each store has. Not affected by the week selector. Identifying consistently strong or weak stores regardless of individual weekly swings
Store drawer: Click any row in the Stores table to open a side panel showing that store's full week-by-week KPI history, a Chart.js score trend, root cause dropdown (Staffing / Training / Technical / Process / Stock / Awareness), and a free-text notes field. Notes are saved to Supabase and visible to all users.
📦SFS — KPIs explained
▶

Ship From Store performance is measured across four KPIs. Each KPI is normalised (0–100) relative to the network for that week, then weighted and combined into a single composite score. Scores are always relative — a store scoring 80 is in the top portion of the network for that week, not necessarily hitting a fixed standard.

% Claimed orders
Percentage of SFS candidate orders that store staff accepted and claimed within the required fulfilment window. This is the most important KPI — if an order isn't claimed, the customer doesn't get it. Stores below the claimed target are flagged red in the table.
Candidate reliability
Of the orders a store claims, what % do they actually fulfil? A store might claim an order, then fail to pack and ship it (item missing, store too busy, etc). High reliability means the store follows through on every claim. Low reliability is a red flag even if claimed % looks good.
Speed — Avg TTC
Average Time To Claim in hours. From the moment an order appears in Onestock to the moment the store accepts it. Faster = better. Stores over the configured speed target are flagged in the table with the exact overage shown. Speed is scored as a ratio of TTC vs the candidate window duration.
Orders per day
Average number of claimed SFS orders processed per active trading day in the selected week. Measures how much a store contributes to overall network volume. Higher-volume stores score better on this dimension — it rewards stores that are actively participating in SFS.
Score = (Claimed% × W1 + Reliability × W2 + Speed × W3 + Volume × W4) / 100
Default weights: W1=35%   W2=20%   W3=25%   W4=20%
All KPI values are normalised 0–100 relative to the network before weighting.
Configurable weights: Admins can adjust the weighting of each KPI from the Settings sidebar (admin password required). Changes can be saved against a specific week, meaning historical scoring uses the weights that were active at the time — not the current ones.
🛍️C&C — KPIs explained
▶

Click & Collect performance tracks whether stores are successfully preparing orders for customer collection and quantifies the revenue impact of failures. The composite score is driven by two weighted KPIs: Time to Bag and % Bagged.

KPI / MetricWhat it measuresWhy it matters
ClaimedNumber of C&C orders that store staff accepted and started processingStarting point. If not claimed, the order won't be prepared for collection — leading to a failed customer visit.
UnclaimedOrders that timed out without being actioned by the storeDirect lost sales. Each unclaimed order is a customer who came to collect and left empty-handed.
UnavailableOrders claimed but then marked unavailable — item couldn't be found in storeIndicates stock accuracy problems. Often caused by shop floor staff selling items allocated to C&C orders.
% Bagged scoredPercentage of C&C orders successfully bagged and ready for customer collectionThe key end-to-end output. High bagged % = the store is executing the full C&C process correctly.
Time to Bag scoredAverage hours from order arrival to the order being bagged and ready for collectionCustomers expect quick turnaround. Long TTB leads to poor collection experience and potential cancellations.
Missed revenueTotal value of orders that were unclaimed or unavailable and not fulfilledThe financial cost of C&C failure. Rows with missed revenue are highlighted red in the table. Aggregated at network level in Health.
Bagged revenueTotal value of orders successfully bagged for collectionThe positive contribution — how much revenue was converted through C&C this week.
Score = (Time-to-Bag score × W1 + Bagged% score × W2) / 100
Default weights: W1=70% TTB   W2=30% Bagged%
TTB is scored inversely — faster stores score higher. Both values normalised 0–100 vs network.
Bag time target: Configurable in the Settings sidebar. Stores exceeding the target are flagged amber in the table with the exact overage shown. The target affects display only — scoring is always relative to the network.
🏆Scores & Tiers
▶

Every store receives a composite score from 0–100 each week, calculated by normalising each KPI relative to the network and applying the configured weights. Scores are then bucketed into four tiers.

A
TOP TIER
Consistently strong across all KPIs. Network benchmarks. These stores should be recognised and used as best practice examples.
B
SOLID
Performing well with minor gaps vs top tier. Stable and reliable. Small targeted improvements could push them into A.
C
DEVELOPING
Underperforming in one or more KPIs. Needs focused support. Use the store drawer to add a root cause and action plan.
D
NEEDS ATTENTION
Significantly below the network average. Urgent intervention required. Three consecutive D-tier weeks triggers a High severity alert.
🛠 Admin password — what it unlocks
Unlock slidersAdjust KPI weights and tier thresholds in the Settings sidebar. Total weights must equal 100%.
Save configLock a scoring configuration against a specific week. Historical weeks will be rescored using the config that was active at that time.
Delete weekPermanently remove a week's data from the database. Irreversible — the original CSV must be re-uploaded to restore.
Delete configRemove a saved scoring configuration from the history panel.
Admin password JDsportsSEA! — built into the tool, not your Supabase login
🔔Alerts explained
▶

Alerts are generated automatically every week for each country. They require no setup — the system checks every store against a set of rules and flags anything meaningful. Requires at least 2 weeks of data loaded.

Alert typeSeverityTrigger condition
3+ consecutive D-tierHighStore has been in Tier D for 3 or more consecutive weeks
Multi-tier dropHighStore drops 2 or more tiers in a single week (e.g. A → C)
Network floor breachHighNetwork average score has been below the configured floor for 3+ consecutive weeks
Single-tier dropMediumStore drops 1 tier week-on-week (e.g. B → C)
Score fall >15ptsMediumScore drops more than 15 points vs prior week without a tier change
Below claimed targetMediumSFS: store's claimed % is below the configured minimum target
Missing storeMediumA store present in the previous week is absent from the current upload
Tier improvementLowStore improves by at least 1 tier — suppressed by default, visible with filter
Tip: Use the week filter on the Alerts tab to see alerts for any specific historical week, not just the latest. Click any alert to jump straight to that store's drawer.
🗑️Deleting a week
▶

If a week was uploaded with incorrect data or the wrong label, it can be permanently deleted from the database. This requires the admin password.

  • Select the week you want to delete from the week dropdown at the top of the programme panel
  • Click the 🗑 Delete week button that appears next to the dropdown
  • Enter the admin password when prompted (JDsportsSEA!)
  • Confirm the permanent deletion in the confirmation dialog
  • The week is removed from the database and the week strip immediately. All other weeks are unaffected.
  • In period mode you can have multiple weeks selected — each will be deleted with its own password confirmation prompt
⚠ Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. The original CSV file must be re-uploaded with the correct week label to restore the data. There is no recycle bin or undo after confirming deletion (only the 10-minute undo window immediately after upload).
Tip: If you uploaded the right data but wrong week label, delete the week and re-upload the same CSV with the correct label. The data itself does not need to be re-exported from Onestock.
JD Sports SEA Fulfilment Hub  ·  Internal Use Only For access or support, contact your ecommerce operations manager
